description: Methodology-focused peer review schema for clinical studies. Deep-dive assessment of study design, bias risks, internal/external validity, and statistical methodology. Used alongside reviewer_full for dual-pass review.

acceptance_dimensions.name: study_design_assessment
acceptance_dimensions.description: Appropriateness of the study design for the research question; adequacy of controls; blinding; randomization; sample size justification.

acceptance_dimensions.name: bias_risk_assessment
acceptance_dimensions.description: Systematic evaluation of selection, performance, detection, attrition, and reporting biases using established frameworks (e.g., Cochrane Risk of Bias 2, ROBINS-I).

acceptance_dimensions.name: internal_validity
acceptance_dimensions.description: Confounding control, measurement validity, temporal relationships, and analytical rigor that support causal inference within the study population.

acceptance_dimensions.name: external_validity
acceptance_dimensions.description: Generalizability of findings to real-world clinical populations; population representativeness; setting applicability.

acceptance_dimensions.name: statistical_method_review
acceptance_dimensions.description: Appropriateness of statistical methods; assumption verification; handling of multiplicity; missing data approach; sensitivity analyses.

bias_risk_categories.description: Risk that the study sample is not representative of the target population, or that allocation was influenced by participant characteristics.
assessment_items: Randomization method is adequate and described
Allocation concealment is maintained
Baseline characteristics are balanced across groups
Inclusion/exclusion criteria are appropriate
Screening and enrollment procedures minimize selection

bias_risk_categories.description: Risk that knowledge of intervention assignment influenced participant behavior or care provider delivery.
assessment_items: Blinding of participants is adequate
Blinding of care providers is adequate
Co-interventions are balanced across groups
Protocol deviations are documented and balanced

bias_risk_categories.description: Risk that knowledge of intervention assignment influenced outcome assessment.
assessment_items: Outcome assessors are blinded
Outcome definitions are objective and pre-specified
Measurement methods are standardized across groups
Timing of outcome assessment is consistent

bias_risk_categories.description: Risk that incomplete outcome data (dropout, loss to follow-up) introduces systematic error.
assessment_items: Dropout rate is reported and acceptable (<20%)
Reasons for dropout are provided and similar across groups
Appropriate missing data method is applied (ITT, multiple imputation)
Sensitivity analyses address potential attrition bias

bias_risk_categories.description: Risk that selective outcome reporting or analysis reporting distorts the evidence.
assessment_items: All pre-specified primary and secondary outcomes are reported
Analysis matches the SAP
Non-significant results are reported, not suppressed
Protocol and SAP are available for comparison
Trial is registered (ClinicalTrials.gov, EudraCT)

internal_validity_score.description: Overall internal validity rating based on bias risk assessment.

score_labels.1: Very low: Critical bias concerns that invalidate causal inference
score_labels.2: Low: Major bias concerns that substantially weaken causal inference
score_labels.3: Moderate: Some bias concerns; causal inference is limited
score_labels.4: High: Minor bias concerns; causal inference is well-supported
score_labels.5: Very high: Minimal bias; causal inference is strong

score_labels.1: Very low: Findings cannot be generalized beyond the study sample
score_labels.2: Low: Limited generalizability due to narrow population or settings
score_labels.3: Moderate: Some generalizability with important caveats
score_labels.4: High: Findings are likely applicable to typical clinical populations
score_labels.5: Very high: Findings are broadly applicable across clinical settings

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Design matches research question; sample size is justified by power calculation; randomization and blinding are appropriate; control group is adequate.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Fundamentally inappropriate design (e.g., using RCT design for a rare disease with no feasible control); no sample size justification; no randomization in an RCT.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Design is appropriate but sample size justification is weak; blinding could be improved; control group has minor issues.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Each bias domain is assessed with specific criteria; overall risk of bias is clearly stated; high-risk domains have mitigating analyses.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: No formal bias assessment performed; multiple high-risk domains with no mitigating analysis; unblinded study with subjective outcomes.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Bias assessment present but incomplete; some domains not addressed; overall risk judgment lacks justification.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Confounders are identified and controlled; measurement instruments are validated; temporal sequence is clear; dose-response or biological plausibility supports causation.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Major confounders uncontrolled; measurement methods not validated; temporal ambiguity undermines causal claims.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Most confounders addressed but some potential confounders remain uncontrolled; measurement methods adequate but not optimal.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Study population represents the intended clinical population; exclusion criteria do not systematically exclude relevant groups; setting is typical of clinical practice.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Highly selected population with no discussion of generalizability; exclusion of major demographic groups without justification.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Population is somewhat narrower than typical clinical practice; some exclusion criteria could limit applicability.

per_dimension_criteria.what_to_look_for: Methods match data type and research question; assumptions are tested; multiplicity is controlled; missing data is handled appropriately; sensitivity analyses are conducted.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_block: Wrong statistical method applied; key assumptions not tested; no multiplicity adjustment when required; no missing data handling; no sensitivity analyses.
per_dimension_criteria.what_triggers_warn: Methods generally appropriate but some assumptions not tested; multiplicity control could be more rigorous; sensitivity analyses are limited.

failure_conditions.expression: any mandatory dimension scores 'block' from either reviewer
all mandatory dimensions score 'pass' from both reviewers and internal_validity_score >= 4 from both
